Code of Conduct

Source: CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md

Our Standards

reportilo is a health research software project. We expect contributors and users to keep discussion professional, evidence-based, and respectful.

Examples of behavior that helps the project:

  • Use welcoming and inclusive language.
  • Focus criticism on code, data, documentation, and methods, not people.
  • State uncertainty clearly when discussing reporting guidance or data quality.
  • Respect different levels of experience with R, Shiny, React, and research reporting guidelines.
  • Accept maintainers’ decisions about scope, release readiness, and moderation.

Examples of unacceptable behavior:

  • Harassment, threats, insults, or discriminatory language.
  • Sexualized language or imagery.
  • Personal attacks, trolling, or sustained disruption.
  • Public or private disclosure of another person’s private information.
  • Knowingly misleading claims about guideline coverage, checklist validation, or research reporting standards.

Scope

This code of conduct applies in all project spaces, including GitHub issues, pull requests, discussions, code review, documentation, and any public or private communication about the project.

Enforcement

Instances of unacceptable behavior may be reported to the project maintainer at the email address listed in DESCRIPTION. Reports will be reviewed as promptly and confidentially as practical.

Maintainers may remove comments, close issues, reject contributions, or block participants when needed to protect the project and its community.

On this page
